description: Accepts HTTP POST requests with name and hour parameters, validates inputs, and returns a time-appropriate greeting or a 400 error with a descriptive message.

trigger.description: Receives JSON body with 'name' (string) and 'hour' (integer 0-23) fields. responseMode is set automatically to responseNode by the Build Agent.

steps.name: Validate Inputs and Compute Greeting
steps.description: Reads name and hour from the POST body, validates presence and types, determines time-of-day period, and sets a valid flag plus error/greeting messages. Falls back to reading $json directly if $json.body is not present, to handle both envelope and flat delivery modes. Wraps all logic in try/catch to return a structured error item on unexpected failures rather than letting the node throw.
steps.error_handling: All logic wrapped in try/catch; unexpected errors return valid:false with a descriptive errorMessage, routing to the 400 error response path rather than crashing the workflow.

steps.name: Check Validation Result
steps.description: Routes to success path if valid is true, otherwise routes to error path. Uses equals comparison against the string 'true' to avoid cross-version boolean operator inconsistencies.

steps.name: Build Success Response
steps.description: Constructs the 200 OK JSON response with greeting, name, hour, and period fields.
steps.error_handling: No external calls; failure is not expected.

steps.name: Respond 200 Success
steps.description: Sends the HTTP 200 response with the greeting JSON body to the caller.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: $json.status, greeting: $json.greeting, name: $json.name, hour: $json.hour, period: $json.period }) }}

steps.name: Build Error Response
steps.description: Constructs the 400 Bad Request JSON response with the descriptive error message.
steps.error_handling: No external calls; failure is not expected.

steps.name: Respond 400 Error
steps.description: Sends the HTTP 400 response with the error JSON body to the caller.

error_handling.on_workflow_failure: Step_1 wraps all logic in try/catch and returns valid:false with a descriptive errorMessage on any unexpected JS error, routing to the 400 error response. This prevents unhandled exceptions from leaving the webhook caller without a response.
error_handling.notes: Because step_1 catches all errors and returns a structured item, the workflow will always reach a respondToWebhook node. A separate error workflow is not required for typical runtime errors.

output.description: Returns 200 with greeting JSON on success via Respond to Webhook node, or 400 with error message JSON on validation failure via Respond to Webhook node. HTTP status codes are explicitly set.

security.pii_handling: Name is a user-provided string; not stored, only echoed back in the response
security.data_flow_notes: All processing is in-memory; no external systems called. The endpoint is intentionally unauthenticated; consider adding n8n Basic Auth or Header Auth via the webhook node's Authentication setting if abuse becomes a concern.
security.authentication_note: No authentication configured. The endpoint is publicly accessible to anyone who knows the URL. Add webhook authentication (Basic Auth or Header Auth) if needed.

cost_estimate: $0.00 per run — no external APIs or LLM calls

test_cases.name: Happy path — evening greeting at midnight (hour 0)
Happy path — late night evening greeting (hour 23)

expected.message: Missing or empty required field: name
Missing or empty required field: name

test_cases.name: Error case — hour out of range (negative)

expected.message: Invalid value for hour: must be a JSON number integer between 0 and 23 (not a string or float)

test_cases.name: Error case — hour out of range (too large)

expected.message: Invalid value for hour: must be a JSON number integer between 0 and 23 (not a string or float)

test_cases.name: Error case — hour is not a number (string)

expected.message: Invalid value for hour: must be a JSON number integer between 0 and 23 (not a string or float)

test_cases.name: Error case — hour is a float (not strict integer)

expected.message: Invalid value for hour: must be a JSON number integer between 0 and 23 (not a string or float)

test_cases.name: Error case — hour is a numeric string (e.g. '9'), must be rejected as not a JSON number type

expected.message: Invalid value for hour: must be a JSON number integer between 0 and 23 (not a string or float)

test_cases.name: Error case — empty body (no name or hour)

expected.message: Missing or empty required field: name
